brand_identity.tagline: The voice of your next career move.
brand_identity.elevator_pitch: Voxa is an AI-first interview coach that simulates realistic, high-pressure conversational interviews. Get instant structured feedback on your tone, pacing, and answers—so you can walk into the real room with absolute confidence.
brand_identity.logo_direction: Minimalist composition using Lucide icons. Combine an `AudioLines` icon with a perfect geometric `Circle`. Stroke weight must be exactly 1.5px for a premium feel. The typography for the logo text 'Voxa' should be set in `Outfit`, font-weight black, tracking-tighter.

typography.hierarchy_rules: H1 max text-5xl/text-6xl, tracking-tighter. Use font-light for subheadings to contrast with font-black main headings. The jump between a heading and its subheading must not exceed 2 Tailwind steps.

colors.guidelines: Base is Midnight Black. Stark White for text. Electric Cyan (174 100% 50%) is used strictly for active states (like the voice orb or subtle hover glows). Do not use AI-slop purple gradients. Primary buttons should be Stark White with Black text.

product_screen_evolution: The new brand explicitly evolves the 'Swiss high-contrast' product screens into a 'Dark-Mode-First High-Contrast' identity. The marketing page will feature deep ambient lighting, glassmorphism, and large typography, while the product screens (/setup, /interview, /report) will seamlessly inherit the new dark CSS variables but retain their dense, cardless, grid-focused functional layouts. This ensures the app feels like one cohesive premium product without breaking the existing utility.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Use Crystal Glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-xl bg-black/60 with 1px border-white/10) for sticky headers and floating elements. Flat solid #0A0A0A with 1px border-border for static cards.
visual_enhancers.motion: Use framer-motion for smooth entrance stagger. The hero voice preview MUST have an animated waveform (using scaleY and delay) or a pulsing glowing orb to signify AI listening.
visual_enhancers.layout: Use generous spacing (p-12 to p-24) for the marketing page. Use the 'Bento' Grid protocol for the feature section (asymmetrical spanning).

components.hero: Headline ('Speak to Your Future'), Subhead, Primary CTA ('Start Mock Interview'), Secondary CTA ('See Sample Report'). Interactive voice preview: an animated mockup of the voice interview with a glowing waveform and fake transcript scrolling.
components.feature_grid: Bento box style grid. At minimum: Voice-first interviewer, Structured scoring, Follow-up questions, Question-by-question feedback.
components.how_it_works: 3-step visual layout (Configure → Speak → Get scored) using cards with Lucide icons (Settings, Mic, FileText).
components.testimonials: 3-6 minimalist testimonial cards. Above them, a scrolling marquee strip of 'Logos of companies our users interview at' (use lucide icons like Github, Trello, Codepen, Figma as placeholders).
components.pricing: 3-tier pricing (Free / Pro / Team). Free is active, Pro/Team should have a 'Join Waitlist' button state.
components.faq: Accordion based FAQ (6-10 Qs) using Shadcn Accordion component.

hero_voice_abstract.description: Vibrant digital audio interface sound wave, perfect for the hero background or voice preview contextual image.

voice_orb_abstract.description: Abstract blue concentric circle pattern, can be used as a background texture for the AI 'speaking' state.

interview_testimonial_1.description: Professional handshake post-interview, suitable for a success story or testimonial avatar/hero.

interview_testimonial_2.description: Man and woman sitting at a table during an interview, for context on the landing page.

instructions_to_main_agent: Redesign the / (landing page) using the brand identity provided.
Implement the Layout with Tailwind CSS and Framer Motion. Ensure components like the Header, Hero, Feature Grid, How it Works, Testimonials, Pricing, and FAQ are built semantically.
Use pure HTML/Tailwind for marketing layouts and Shadcn for interactive pieces (Accordions, Buttons).
Replace 'Inter' entirely. Use 'Outfit' for headings and 'DM Sans' for body text. Add the Google Fonts link in index.html.
Update `tailwind.config.js` and `index.css` with the new color variables and fonts.

For the Interactive Voice Preview in the Hero, build an animated mockup showing a pulsing orb or waveform (using lucide-react 'AudioLines' or custom CSS animation) and some placeholder transcript text scrolling upwards to mimic a real interview.
Make sure all interactive and key informational elements have a `data-testid` attribute (e.g. `data-testid="hero-cta-button"`).
In the pricing section, configure the Free tier with a primary action, and the Pro/Team tiers as 'Join Waitlist'.
Update the product pages (/setup, /interview, /report) visually to inherit the new dark CSS variables. Ensure text contrast remains accessible.
Do NOT use transparent backgrounds for Headers or Overlay sections. Use glassmorphism.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) for sticky navs and floating cards.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., transition: all). Apply them explicitly.
You MUST NOT center align the app container. Text left-aligned reads better.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters (🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯) for icons. Use lucide-react.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures or noise overlays can elevate the look.
